scenarios.scenario: A rep PATCHes revenueCents on a lead they don't own (via /api/leads/[id])
scenarios.rationale: The PATCH handler is unchanged in gate order: leadUpdateSchema.safeParse -> getLead(id, user.id, user.role) (visibility-scoped; returns null/404 if the rep cannot see the lead) -> canEditLead(me, existing) (manager/super_manager -> true; unowned lead -> true; else ownerId === user.id, else 403 Forbidden) -> updateLead. revenueCents/currency are forwarded the SAME way as every other optional field on this schema — no new bypass path, no separate revenue-only endpoint. A rep who does not own the lead gets 403 before updateLead is ever called.

scenarios.scenario: Inline revenue PATCH omits `name` and attempts to bypass leadUpdateSchema validation
scenarios.rationale: leadUpdateSchema requires name (min length 1). The inline editor's saveRevenue() function always sends { name: lead.name, revenueCents, currency } — name is read from the already-loaded lead record, never omitted. A bare { revenueCents } payload would 400 before reaching canEditLead; the implementation avoids this by construction (see src/routes/leads/[id]/+page.svelte saveRevenue()).

scenarios.scenario: Moving a lead to `done` clobbers won-stage financial metadata (dealValueCents/wonOrgName/signedAt/lostReason)
scenarios.rationale: moveLeadStage has a dedicated `else if (stage === 'done')` branch inserted BEFORE the generic clearing branch (which nulls those fields for any other non-won/non-lost transition). The done branch's SET clause only touches stage/revenueCents/currency/lastActivityAt/updatedAt. Verified via the new pipeline-db.spec.ts 'does NOT touch dealValueCents/wonOrgName when moving a won lead to done' test (Hybrid, self-skips without live Postgres, but the code path is structurally isolated from the generic branch by construction — confirmed by source read during PVL cycle 1).

scenarios.scenario: A lead double-counts revenue on the dashboard after moving from `done` to another stage and back
scenarios.rationale: getRevenuePerAe adds eq(crmLeads.stage, 'done') to its WHERE clause (E2 current-stage guard) — only leads whose CURRENT stage is 'done' contribute to the sum, regardless of how many historical stage->'done' transition rows exist (selectDistinctOn picks only the latest per lead, and the current-stage guard additionally excludes leads no longer in 'done'). A lead that transitioned to done and later moved out is excluded even though its revenueCents value is not nulled by the generic clearing branch.

scenarios.scenario: Migration 0035 is accidentally applied to a live/shared database in this environment
scenarios.rationale: bun run db:migrate was NOT invoked at any point in this EXECUTE pass. The migration file itself could not even be generated in this session due to a pre-existing, unrelated drizzle snapshot-chain collision (documented in the backlog note); no SQL was applied anywhere. The plan's hard-stop constraint ('Do NOT apply migration 0035 to any live DB in this env') was honored trivially — no migration exists yet to apply.
